claim_text: Teal evaluates a learning-accelerated WAN TE method on traffic matrices from Microsoft WAN traces.
confidence_basis: 1 peer-reviewed source; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The SIGCOMM paper states the evaluation uses Microsoft WAN traffic matrices.

limitations: Not a public production deployment of RL control.
Not a public production deployment of RL control.

claim_text: On a large WAN topology, Teal reports higher satisfied demand and faster runtime than other TE acceleration schemes in its benchmark.
confidence_basis: 1 peer-reviewed source; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The located Teal evaluation reports demand-satisfaction and speedup comparisons.

support_scope.dataset_or_benchmark: Microsoft WAN traces; large WAN topology above 1,700 nodes
limitations: The claim is limited to the paper's benchmark setup.
The claim is limited to the paper's benchmark setup.

claim_text: Teal uses learned allocation followed by constrained optimization rather than a pure RL controller.
confidence_basis: 1 peer-reviewed source; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The design section describes multi-agent RL plus ADMM fine tuning for constraints.

limitations: Does not show production enforcement behavior.
Does not show production enforcement behavior.

claim_text: CFR-RL combines RL-based critical-flow selection with an optimization step for rerouting selected flows.
confidence_basis: 1 peer-reviewed source; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The abstract and design describe RL selection plus rerouting optimization.

limitations: Topology and traffic conditions are paper-specific.
Topology and traffic conditions are paper-specific.

claim_text: CFR-RL uses a linear-programming rerouting subproblem after selecting critical flows.
confidence_basis: 1 peer-reviewed source; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The paper describes adopting LP to solve the selected-flow rerouting problem.

limitations: LP solve details are scoped to selected flows.
LP solve details are scoped to selected flows.

claim_text: B4 is a production WAN TE deployment built around centralized control rather than an RL controller.
confidence_basis: 1 peer-reviewed source; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The production-experience paper identifies B4 as a globally deployed software-defined WAN with centralized TE.

limitations: Google-specific workload and topology.
Google-specific workload and topology.

claim_text: B4 reports long-running link utilization improvements under a centralized TE design.
confidence_basis: 1 peer-reviewed source; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The B4 paper reports high utilization and long-term deployment experience.

limitations: Applicability depends on elastic internal traffic.
Applicability depends on elastic internal traffic.

claim_text: B4 kept a fallback path by allowing centralized TE to be disabled in favor of shortest-path forwarding.
confidence_basis: 1 peer-reviewed source; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The B4 architecture section describes disabling TE and falling back to shortest-path forwarding.

limitations: Does not evaluate RL fallback.
Does not evaluate RL fallback.

claim_text: SWAN treats congestion-free updates as an explicit operational requirement for inter-datacenter TE.
confidence_basis: 1 peer-reviewed source; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The SWAN abstract frames frequent data-plane reconfiguration and congestion-free transition as a core problem.

limitations: Evaluation includes prototype and simulations, not fully autonomous RL.
Evaluation includes prototype and simulations, not fully autonomous RL.

claim_text: SWAN uses planned update sequences and scratch capacity to avoid transient congestion during TE reconfiguration.
confidence_basis: 1 peer-reviewed source; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The design text describes scratch capacity and congestion-free or bounded-congestion update plans.

limitations: Specific to SWAN's assumptions and traffic classes.
Specific to SWAN's assumptions and traffic classes.

claim_text: For low-risk production TE, RL is better framed as a bounded proposer or accelerator than as the sole authority for constraint enforcement.
confidence_basis: 3 peer-reviewed sources; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: Teal retains constrained optimization while B4 and SWAN emphasize fallback and safe transition plans.

limitations: This is a synthesis recommendation, not a direct source quote.
This is a synthesis recommendation, not a direct source quote.

confidence_basis: 1 run-log source; direct support on full text

claim_text: Safe RL literature treats safety constraints and risky exploration as central issues rather than solved deployment details.
confidence_basis: 1 peer-reviewed source; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The survey defines Safe RL around performance and safety constraints during learning or deployment.

limitations: Not networking-specific.
Not networking-specific.

claim_text: Safe RL methods include risk-sensitive and constrained criteria, which implies that reward maximization alone is insufficient for safety-critical network control.
confidence_basis: 1 peer-reviewed source; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The survey taxonomy includes risk-sensitive and constrained criteria.

support_scope.population_or_domain: safe RL methods applied by analogy to network control
limitations: Application to TE requires engineering validation.
Application to TE requires engineering validation.

claim_text: Exploration strategies that ignore risk are a poor fit for live TE systems with SLA obligations.
confidence_basis: 2 peer-reviewed sources; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: Safe RL discusses risky exploration, while RFC 2702 frames TE as performance optimization for large backbones.

limitations: A network-specific safe-RL deployment study would strengthen this.
A network-specific safe-RL deployment study would strengthen this.

claim_text: Claims that RL generalizes from traffic-matrix simulations to production TE should be treated as contested until validated under failure and update conditions.
confidence_basis: 2 peer-reviewed sources; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: Teal is benchmark evidence, while SWAN shows update safety conditions matter operationally.

limitations: Requires a direct sim-to-real TE study.
Requires a direct sim-to-real TE study.

claim_text: Black-box RL decisions create audit friction unless paired with explicit constraints, fallback, and human-readable policy checks.
confidence_basis: 3 peer-reviewed sources; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The sources support fallback, transition safety, and constrained safety framing.

limitations: Direct audit case studies for RL TE were not retrieved.
Direct audit case studies for RL TE were not retrieved.

claim_text: Classical network-flow formulations remain cheap to operate when the problem fits minimum-cost-flow or LP-style structure.
confidence_basis: 2 official sources; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: OR-Tools documents minimum-cost-flow structure and RFC 2702 frames TE as resource optimization.

evidence_locator.paragraph_hint: OR-Tools min cost flow page summary

limitations: Does not cover very large path-based WAN TE instances.
Does not cover very large path-based WAN TE instances.

claim_text: Teal's reported speedups create a plausible ROI case for learning-accelerated optimization when LP runtime becomes the bottleneck.
confidence_basis: 1 peer-reviewed source; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The Teal benchmark reports large runtime gains and demand-satisfaction improvements.

support_scope.dataset_or_benchmark: Microsoft WAN traces; large topology above 1,700 nodes
support_scope.comparison_baseline: production optimization engine and acceleration schemes
limitations: ROI depends on engineering cost, retraining cost, and incident risk.
ROI depends on engineering cost, retraining cost, and incident risk.

claim_text: Replacing classical solvers with RL-primary control has weaker economic support than using RL to reduce solve time or operator workload.
confidence_basis: 3 peer-reviewed sources; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The strongest ML evidence is acceleration, while production TE sources emphasize fallback and update safety.

limitations: Needs local cost model.
Needs local cost model.

claim_text: Engineering effort should first buy observability, replay simulation, and fallback automation before buying an RL-primary controller.
confidence_basis: 3 peer-reviewed sources; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: Production TE and safe RL evidence points to fallback, update safety, and safety constraints as prerequisites.

limitations: Does not price vendor tools.
Does not price vendor tools.

claim_text: A pilot should measure objective value against a strong LP/MIP baseline rather than only against heuristics.
confidence_basis: 3 peer-reviewed sources; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: Teal and CFR-RL both retain solver comparisons or solver components, and OR-Tools documents classical flow optimization.

limitations: Exact baseline depends on local TE formulation.
Exact baseline depends on local TE formulation.

claim_text: MPLS TE requirements were framed around resource optimization and congestion management before modern RL controllers.
confidence_basis: 1 official source; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: RFC 2702 describes TE as optimization of resource use and traffic performance.

limitations: Standards text is not a benchmark.
Standards text is not a benchmark.

claim_text: Production SDN TE adoption favored hybrid operation and fallback rather than one-shot replacement of existing routing mechanisms.
confidence_basis: 2 peer-reviewed sources; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: B4 layered TE over routing with fallback, and SWAN designed safe transition plans.

limitations: Two cases do not cover every provider.
Two cases do not cover every provider.

claim_text: The recurring pattern in network control is that new control-plane techniques survive by becoming constrained subsystems.
confidence_basis: 3 peer-reviewed sources; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: Teal uses constrained optimization, B4 uses fallback, and SWAN uses constrained update plans.

limitations: Historical generalization is based on a small set of high-signal cases.
Historical generalization is based on a small set of high-signal cases.

claim_text: A defensible near-term analogy for RL in TE is learning-accelerated optimization, not autonomous replacement of MPLS/SDN TE discipline.
confidence_basis: 2 peer-reviewed sources; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: The modern ML paper and older TE requirements align on optimization with constraints.

limitations: Future safe-RL deployment evidence could change this.
Future safe-RL deployment evidence could change this.

claim_text: A credible production trial needs ECMP or shortest-path fallback before exposing RL proposals to live traffic.
confidence_basis: 2 peer-reviewed sources; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: B4 supports fallback, and CFR-RL assumes default ECMP with only selected rerouting.

limitations: Fallback implementation differs by network.
Fallback implementation differs by network.

claim_text: The strongest retrieved RL-for-TE evidence supports augmentation of optimization loops, not full replacement of classical solvers.
confidence_basis: 2 peer-reviewed sources; direct support on full text

content_verification.entailment_rationale: Both key RL sources combine learning with constrained or LP optimization.

limitations: The retrieved set is not exhaustive.
